text: Recommendation: don't fight for "orchestration" (it's saturated and framework-coded). "Coordination layer" or plain-language ("agents that know who's who") is closer to open ground. [...] - Avoid: general "orchestration," "swarm," and "framework" positioning — those lanes are extremely crowded (LangGraph/CrewAI/AutoGen/OpenAI/~40 worktree tools) and none of it is where tenex-edge's actual differentiation lives anyway. [...] - "orchestration" / "orchestrator" (nearly every tool name/tagline uses it — Conductor, Composio Agent Orchestrator, "9 Open-Source Agent Orchestrators," etc. — it now signals "yet another dashboard.") [...] 1. **The category is crowded (~90+ tools) and "orchestration"/"swarm" are saturated and framework-coded — avoid them entirely.** [...] **Fresh framing that could stand out:** language of *citizenship, presence, and social fact* rather than *orchestration and control* — "agents that know who's in the room," "an agent's identity survives the terminal it was born in," "coordination without a control tower." Avoid personifying too far into "AI teammates" (Claude Code already owns that framing natively). [...] 5. **Fresh, ownable register:** citizenship / presence / *"an agent's identity survives the terminal it was born in"* / *"coordination without a control tower."*
